lines: A note pinned at the mouth of the first gallery. The niches behind it are packed with the fevered dead, jaw to heel, years gone to bone.
'The sick go here. Fever, gold in the eye, the early opening. I kept them at the litany to the end, because a body that prays opens slower, and a slow opening is a quiet one.'

'Most of these closed back to clean bone. Some did not. The ones with the gold still in the seams I sealed deepest and did not open again. I will not write what they were turning into. The cold did not take it. The cold holds it, mid-turn, hard and pale and not finished, and some part of it is still in there. I have heard one work its jaw against the stone, slow, in the dark. I do not go back to that gallery.'
